evals.name: error-handling-tagged
evals.prompt: I need to create an error for when a user is not found in the database. I'll just throw a regular Error with a message. Write me a function that fetches a user by ID and throws if not found.
evals.expected_output: Should redirect to Data.TaggedError with typed error channel instead of throwing.

assertions.text: Does not use throw or new Error for expected failures
Uses Effect.gen with yield* for control flow
Error is yieldable without Effect.fail wrapper
assertions.description: Tagged errors should be yielded directly (yield* new UserNotFound) not wrapped in Effect.fail

evals.name: service-layer-design
evals.prompt: Create a UserRepository service in Effect that depends on a database connection. The service should have findById and save methods. Show me how to wire it all up.
evals.expected_output: Should use Context.Tag, Layer composition, and provide once at entry point.

assertions.text: Uses Context.Tag to define the service
Service methods return Effect with R = never (no leaked dependencies)
assertions.description: Service method return types should not include other services in their R parameter

assertions.text: Uses Layer.effect or Layer.scoped for implementation
Provides layers at the entry point, not scattered
assertions.description: Effect.provide should be called once at the program entry point, not inside service methods

assertions.text: Uses NodeRuntime.runMain or Effect.runFork at entry

evals.name: resource-scoping
evals.prompt: Write an Effect program that opens a database connection, runs a query, and ensures the connection is closed. Use acquireRelease.
evals.expected_output: Should wrap acquireRelease in Effect.scoped to prevent resource leaks.

assertions.text: Wraps in Effect.scoped to close the scope
Does not use try/finally for resource cleanup
Uses Effect.gen for the program body

evals.name: concurrent-processing
evals.prompt: I have an array of 100 URLs to fetch. Write me an Effect program that fetches all of them concurrently but limits to 10 at a time.
evals.expected_output: Should use Effect.forEach with concurrency option, not raw fork/fiber management.

assertions.text: Uses Effect.forEach or Effect.all with concurrency option
Does not manually manage fibers for this use case
Uses Effect.tryPromise or HttpClient for fetching
Uses tagged error for fetch failures
